commands.name: update-config
commands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool. (bundled)

commands.name: debug
commands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues (bundled)

commands.name: simplify
commands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found. (bundled)

commands.name: batch
commands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR. (bundled)

commands.name: loop
commands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) (bundled)

commands.name: schedule
commands.description: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. (bundled)

commands.name: claude-api
commands.description:
Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks. (bundled)

commands.name: compact
commands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]

commands.name: context
commands.description: Show current context usage

commands.name: cost
commands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session

commands.name: heapdump
commands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

commands.name: review
commands.description: Review a pull request

commands.name: security-review
commands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

commands.name: extra-usage
commands.description: Configure extra usage to keep working when limits are hit

commands.name: insights
commands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions

agents.name: general-purpose
agents.description: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you.

agents.name: statusline-setup
agents.description: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting.

agents.name: Explore
agents.description: Fast agent specialized for exploring codebases. Use this when you need to quickly find files by patterns (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), search code for keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer questions about the codebase (eg. "how do API endpoints work?"). When calling this agent, specify the desired thoroughness level: "quick" for basic searches, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" for comprehensive analysis across multiple locations and naming conventions.

agents.name: Plan
agents.description: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs.

models.description: Opus 4.6 with 1M context · Most capable for complex work

message.content: 来一个长一点的 yaml，100 行

content.thinking: The user wants a longer YAML file, about 100 lines. Let me create one with the Write tool since they asked me to use it. Let me generate a reasonable YAML config file.

input.content:
app:
  name: my-application
  version: 2.1.0
  description: A sample web application
  author: dev-team
  license: MIT

server:
  host: 0.0.0.0
  port: 8080
  timeout: 30s
  max_connections: 1000
  keep_alive: true
  cors:
    enabled: true
    origins:
      - http://localhost:3000
      - https://example.com
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - DELETE
    headers:
      - Content-Type
      - Authorization

database:
  primary:
    driver: postgres
    host: localhost
    port: 5432
    name: app_db
    username: admin
    pool_size: 20
    max_idle: 5
    ssl_mode: require
  redis:
    host: localhost
    port: 6379
    db: 0
    max_retries: 3
    pool_size: 10

logging:
  level: info
  format: json
  output: stdout
  file:
    enabled: true
    path: /var/log/app/app.log
    max_size: 100MB
    max_age: 30
    max_backups: 10
    compress: true

auth:
  jwt:
    secret_key: change-me-in-production
    expiration: 3600
    refresh_expiration: 86400
    issuer: my-application
  oauth:
    google:
      enabled: true
      client_id: google-client-id
      scopes:
        - openid
        - email
        - profile
    github:
      enabled: false
      client_id: github-client-id
      scopes:
        - user
        - repo

cache:
  enabled: true
  ttl: 300
  max_entries: 10000
  eviction_policy: lru

monitoring:
  metrics:
    enabled: true
    path: /metrics
    port: 9090
  health:
    enabled: true
    path: /health
    interval: 10s
  tracing:
    enabled: false
    provider: jaeger
    endpoint: http://localhost:14268
    sample_rate: 0.1

features:
  rate_limiting:
    enabled: true
    requests_per_second: 100
    burst: 200
  notifications:
    email: true
    slack: false
    webhook: true
  maintenance_mode: false
  dark_mode: true
  beta_features:
    - new_dashboard
    - advanced_search
    - bulk_export

    github:
      enabled: false
      client_id: github-client-id
      scopes:
        - user
        - repo

cache:
  enabled: true
  ttl: 300
  max_entries: 10000
  eviction_policy: lru

monitoring:
  metrics:
    enabled: true
    path: /metrics
    port: 9090
  health:
    enabled: true
    path: /health
    interval: 10s
  tracing:
    enabled: false
    provider: jaeger
    endpoint: http://localhost:14268
    sample_rate: 0.1

features:
  rate_limiting:
    enabled: true
    requests_per_second: 100
    burst: 200
  notifications:
    email: true
    slack: false
    webhook: true
  maintenance_mode: false
  dark_mode: true
  beta_features:
    - new_dashboard
    - advanced_search
    - bulk_export

tool_use_result.content:
app:
  name: my-application
  version: 2.1.0
  description: A sample web application
  author: dev-team
  license: MIT

server:
  host: 0.0.0.0
  port: 8080
  timeout: 30s
  max_connections: 1000
  keep_alive: true
  cors:
    enabled: true
    origins:
      - http://localhost:3000
      - https://example.com
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - DELETE
    headers:
      - Content-Type
      - Authorization

database:
  primary:
    driver: postgres
    host: localhost
    port: 5432
    name: app_db
    username: admin
    pool_size: 20
    max_idle: 5
    ssl_mode: require
  redis:
    host: localhost
    port: 6379
    db: 0
    max_retries: 3
    pool_size: 10

logging:
  level: info
  format: json
  output: stdout
  file:
    enabled: true
    path: /var/log/app/app.log
    max_size: 100MB
    max_age: 30
    max_backups: 10
    compress: true

auth:
  jwt:
    secret_key: change-me-in-production
    expiration: 3600
    refresh_expiration: 86400
    issuer: my-application
  oauth:
    google:
      enabled: true
      client_id: google-client-id
      scopes:
        - openid
        - email
        - profile
    github:
      enabled: false
      client_id: github-client-id
      scopes:
        - user
        - repo

cache:
  enabled: true
  ttl: 300
  max_entries: 10000
  eviction_policy: lru

monitoring:
  metrics:
    enabled: true
    path: /metrics
    port: 9090
  health:
    enabled: true
    path: /health
    interval: 10s
  tracing:
    enabled: false
    provider: jaeger
    endpoint: http://localhost:14268
    sample_rate: 0.1

features:
  rate_limiting:
    enabled: true
    requests_per_second: 100
    burst: 200
  notifications:
    email: true
    slack: false
    webhook: true
  maintenance_mode: false
  dark_mode: true
  beta_features:
    - new_dashboard
    - advanced_search
    - bulk_export

content.text: 已创建 `app.yaml`，共 100 行，包含了服务器、数据库、日志、认证、缓存、监控和功能开关等配置项。

result: 已创建 `app.yaml`，共 100 行，包含了服务器、数据库、日志、认证、缓存、监控和功能开关等配置项。

commands.name: update-config
commands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool. (bundled)

commands.name: debug
commands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues (bundled)

commands.name: simplify
commands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found. (bundled)

commands.name: batch
commands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR. (bundled)

commands.name: loop
commands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) (bundled)

commands.name: schedule
commands.description: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. (bundled)

commands.name: claude-api
commands.description:
Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks. (bundled)

commands.name: compact
commands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]

commands.name: context
commands.description: Show current context usage

commands.name: cost
commands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session

commands.name: heapdump
commands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

commands.name: review
commands.description: Review a pull request

commands.name: security-review
commands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

commands.name: extra-usage
commands.description: Configure extra usage to keep working when limits are hit

commands.name: insights
commands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions

agents.name: general-purpose
agents.description: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you.

agents.name: statusline-setup
agents.description: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting.

agents.name: Explore
agents.description: Fast agent specialized for exploring codebases. Use this when you need to quickly find files by patterns (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), search code for keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer questions about the codebase (eg. "how do API endpoints work?"). When calling this agent, specify the desired thoroughness level: "quick" for basic searches, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" for comprehensive analysis across multiple locations and naming conventions.

agents.name: Plan
agents.description: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs.

models.description: Opus 4.6 with 1M context · Most capable for complex work

input.content:
project:
  name: cloud-platform
  version: 3.5.2
  environment: staging
  region: ap-southeast-1

services:
  api-gateway:
    replicas: 3
    port: 443
    protocol: https
    resources:
      cpu: 500m
      memory: 512Mi
    health_check:
      path: /ping
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
  user-service:
    replicas: 2
    port: 8001
    resources:
      cpu: 250m
      memory: 256Mi
    dependencies:
      - postgres
      - redis
  order-service:
    replicas: 4
    port: 8002
    resources:
      cpu: 1000m
      memory: 1Gi
    dependencies:
      - postgres
      - kafka
  notification-service:
    replicas: 1
    port: 8003
    resources:
      cpu: 100m
      memory: 128Mi
    dependencies:
      - redis
      - smtp

infrastructure:
  postgres:
    version: "15"
    host: db.internal
    port: 5432
    databases:
      - users
      - orders
      - analytics
    backup:
      enabled: true
      schedule: "0 2 * * *"
      retention_days: 30
  redis:
    version: "7"
    host: cache.internal
    port: 6379
    cluster: true
    nodes: 3
  kafka:
    version: "3.4"
    brokers:
      - kafka-0.internal:9092
      - kafka-1.internal:9092
      - kafka-2.internal:9092
    topics:
      - name: order-events
        partitions: 12
        replication: 3
      - name: user-events
        partitions: 6
        replication: 3
      - name: notifications
        partitions: 3
        replication: 2

networking:
  load_balancer:
    type: alb
    scheme: internet-facing
    idle_timeout: 60
  dns:
    zone: example.com
    records:
      - name: api
        type: A
        ttl: 300
      - name: app
        type: CNAME
        ttl: 600

alerts:
  channels:
    - type: slack
      webhook: https://hooks.slack.com/xxx
    - type: email
      recipients:
        - ops@example.com
        - dev@example.com
  rules:
    - name: high-cpu
      condition: cpu > 80%
      duration: 5m
      severity: warning
    - name: service-down
      condition: health_check_failed
      duration: 1m
      severity: critical
    - name: disk-space
      condition: disk > 90%
      duration: 10m
      severity: warning
